overview: Flux is the most technically photographic of the image models. It rewards prompts that read like a photography brief — camera body, lens, focal length, lighting setup, film stock. The r/StableDiffusion and Civitai communities have extensively mapped its vocabulary: specific equipment references trigger the corresponding aesthetic almost instantly. It also takes negative prompts seriously, which no other major model does as reliably.

style_specificity.description: Camera body, lens, film stock — these single terms unlock huge style shifts in Flux.

technical_photography.description: Photography technical terms that map to real lens/sensor physics.

lighting.description: Flux responds to both studio and natural lighting setups described precisely.

negative_prompt.description: Flux is one of the few models that genuinely uses negative prompts. Always add one.

subject_detail.description: Flux rewards verbosity unlike MJ. Describe clothing texture, material, background details.

power_patterns: Portrait photograph of [subject], [clothing/materials], [location], [lighting setup], shot on [camera], [lens], [film/sensor], [color grade]. Negative: [exclusions].
Product photography: [item description], [surface/backdrop], [lighting direction and quality], [camera/lens], sharp focus, [style]. Negative: no text, no hands.
antipatterns: No negative prompt — Flux takes it seriously and outputs improve consistently when you tell it what to avoid.
Vague style references — 'artistic' or 'beautiful' do nothing. 'Shot on Hasselblad, medium format, soft' does everything.
Skipping lens/camera — these are the fastest style lever in Flux and most prompts leave them out.
Conflicting technical terms — 'f/1.4 bokeh' AND 'everything sharp throughout' confuses the depth of field simulation.

strong: Environmental portrait of a chef in a commercial kitchen, flour on apron, intense expression, harsh overhead fluorescent mixed with window fill light, shot on Canon 5D Mark IV, 35mm f/2, ISO 1600, grain. Negative: no text, no other people in frame.

Product photography: a glass perfume bottle, black velvet surface, single spotlight from above-left, long shadow, deep teal background, shot on Hasselblad, 100mm macro, perfectly sharp, commercial quality. Negative: no dust, no reflections of crew.
weak: A nice photo of a chef cooking.
A beautiful perfume bottle on a dark background.
